what_it_does: Renders a conditional full-screen modal region that displays a selected workshop node's type, id, address, and full output, with a close control.
observations: Conditionally returns null when no workshop item is active.
Renders a fixed-position modal container with className 'workshop' and data-ui-ref='workshop'.
Provides a span-based close affordance that calls setWorkshop(null).
Uses kit components SectionHead, Badge, and Surface for the title, address, and output well.
Displays the node type as the SectionHead title, nodeId as the tag, address in a dim Badge, and output inside a Surface with tone 'sig'.
standouts: data-ui-ref='workshop' is preserved as the resolveUiTarget keystone.
Adopts the shared kit vocabulary (SectionHead/Badge/Surface) even though there is no modal exemplar.
Uses the 'display voice' pattern: node-type as title, node-id as kicker, address as dim metadata.
conventions: Follows the kit-based shared component vocabulary.
Uses className for structural/layout chrome rather than look.
Uses a functional React component with early-return guard for null state.
Imports from relative paths and consumes context via a hook.
concerns: The close control is a <span> with onClick, not a button, which may hurt accessibility.
No visible modal accessibility attributes such as role='dialog', aria-modal, or focus management.
No visible handling for Escape key or click-outside-to-close.
The .workshop, .close, .ws-output, and .ws-full classNames are referenced but their styles are not shown in this file.
notes: A kit-styled full-screen output viewer modal that preserves the original data-ui-ref and open/close behavior while wrapping content in shared SectionHead/Badge/Surface components.
questions: What is the exact shape of the workshop object provided by AppContext?
How is the 'workshop' UI ref resolved and opened elsewhere?
Where are the CSS rules for .workshop, .close, .ws-output, and .ws-full defined?
Is there intentional accessibility behavior (focus trap, Escape to close) handled outside this file?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a full-screen output viewer modal opened via the workshop UI ref; the file matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Shared kit vocabulary over bespoke markup.
Preservation of existing behavior during redesign.
Minimal structural chrome, reusable surface components.
intention_signals: Elevate a bare prototype modal into a consistent commander's-bridge interface by composing kit primitives while keeping the original modal mechanics intact.
contribution: Provides a modal region implementation that a fused system would keep for its integration point (data-ui-ref='workshop'), its use of shared kit components, and its encapsulation of the full output viewer layout.
summary_for_embedding: Workshop.tsx is a conditional full-screen modal region that displays a selected node's type, id, address, and full output using kit SectionHead, Badge, and Surface components.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a consistent, kit-styled full-screen output viewer modal triggered by the workshop UI ref.

symbols.does: Renders the full-screen workshop modal when a workshop item is active.
symbols.description: Workshop is a functional React component exported from Workshop.tsx. It reads the active workshop state from AppContext and returns null when none is set. When a workshop item exists, it renders a fixed-position modal container with a close control, a SectionHead identifying the node by type, id, and address, and a Surface well containing the node's full output text.
